feature.name: Ranger Archetype: Griffon Scout (totom)
feature.text: The elves of the Griffon Court are practical and disciplined, well suited to surviving in their isolated holding on a windswept crag jutting out of the Margreve. Trained by elven court veterans, rangers of the Griffon Scout archetype learn to move swiftly and stealthily both on foot and while mounted, gathering information on their enemies or delivering devastating hit-and-run raids with precision and coordination.
Tales Of The Old Margreve - Players Guide

feature.name: Griffon Scout Magic (Griffon Scout) (totom)
feature.text: Starting at 3rd level, you learn an additional spell when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown in the Griffon Scout Spells table. The spell counts as a ranger spell for you, but it doesn’t count against the number of ranger spells you know.
Ranger Level | Spells
3rd | featherfall
5th | find steed
9th | haste
13th | greater invisibility
17th | telepathic bond
Tales Of The Old Margreve - Players Guide

feature.name: Mounted Scout (Griffon Scout) (totom)
feature.text: At 3rd level, you have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks while mounted, and your Dexterity (Stealth) checks apply to both you and your mount.
Tales Of The Old Margreve - Players Guide

feature.name: Skirmishers Step (Griffon Scout) (totom)
feature.text: At 3rd level, when you move at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and hit it with a melee weapon attack in the same turn, that attack deals an extra 1d8 damage of the same type dealt by the weapon to the target, and, when you or your mount move out of the target’s reach before the end of your next turn, you don’t provoke an opportunity attack from the target.
Tales Of The Old Margreve - Players Guide

feature.name: Coordinated Strikes (Griffon Scout) (totom)
feature.text: Starting at 7th level, you can deliver punishing flurries of attacks in perfect coordination with your allies. When one of your allies hits a target within 30 feet of you with an attack, you can use your reaction to make one weapon attack against that target.
Tales Of The Old Margreve - Players Guide

feature.name: Griffon Wings (Griffon Scout) (totom)
feature.text: At 11th level, when you cast find steed, you can choose for your steed to take the form of a griffon, though it is a fey instead of a monstrosity.
Tales Of The Old Margreve - Players Guide

feature.name: Strike and Fade (Griffon Scout) (totom)
feature.text: Starting at 15th level, when you or your mount move at least 20 feet during your turn, you become an evasive blur. Until the start of your next turn, a creature that attacks you has disadvantage on the first attack roll it makes against you or your mount.
Tales Of The Old Margreve - Players Guide

feature.name: Ranger Archetype: Grove Warden (totom)
feature.text: Fiercely protective of their territory, the alseid herds form a close bond with the Margreve. In return for their diligent protection, the forest offered its blessing to dedicated alseid rangers. In recent years, woodsy adventurers of other races who have earned the alseids’ trust also received this blessing, though their numbers are scant. These Grove Wardens can tap into the living magic of the Margreve and call upon its aid. Their senses travel through the plants and earth of the woods, and the very land rises up to strike down the ranger’s enemies.
Tales Of The Old Margreve - Players Guide

feature.name: Grove Warden Magic (Grove Warden) (totom)
feature.text: Starting at 3rd level, you learn an additional spell when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown in the Grove Warden Spells table. The spell counts as a ranger spell for you, but it doesn’t count against the number of ranger spells you know.
Ranger Level | Spells
3rd | entangle
5th | branding smite
9th | speak with plants
13th | hallucinatory terrain
17th | animate objects
Tales Of The Old Margreve - Players Guide

feature.name: Whispers of the Forest (Grove Warden) (totom)
feature.text: At 3rd level, when you use your Primeval Awareness feature while within a forest, you add humanoids to the list of creature types you can sense. When sensing humanoids, you know the general direction of the creatures, and you know if a humanoid is solitary, in a small group of up to 5 humanoids, or a pack of more than 5 humanoids.
Tales Of The Old Margreve - Players Guide

feature.name: Margreves Will (Grove Warden) (totom)
feature.text: At 3rd level, you can magically draw on the living essence of the land to hamper your foes. As a bonus action, choose one creature you can see within 60 feet of you. Your next weapon attack against that creature has advantage. If that attack hits, the creature’s speed is reduced by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. When you reach 11th level in this class, if that attack hits, the creature’s speed is instead halved until the start of your next turn.
Tales Of The Old Margreve - Players Guide

feature.name: Intruders Bane (Grove Warden) (totom)
feature.text: At 7th level, you can command the land around you to come to your aid. As a bonus action, choose a point you can see on the ground within 60 feet. You cause the area within 15 feet of that point to undulate and warp. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failure, a creature is pushed up to 15 feet in a direction of your choice and knocked prone.
Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.
Tales Of The Old Margreve - Players Guide

feature.name: Wrath of the Forest (Grove Warden) (totom)
feature.text: At 11th level, you can call on the land in your vicinity to strike at your enemies. When you take the Attack action, you can use a bonus action to make a rock, branch, root, or other small object attack a creature within 30 feet of you. You are proficient with the attack, it counts as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage, and you add your Dexterity modifier to the attack and damage rolls. The damage is of a type appropriate to the surroundings and the damage die is a d8.
Tales Of The Old Margreve - Players Guide

feature.name: Living Bulwark (Grove Warden) (totom)
feature.text: Starting at 15th level, the land around you comes to your aid when you are danger, interposing rocks, branches, vines, roots, or even the ground itself between you and your foes. When a creature you can see targets you with an attack, you can use your reaction to roll a d8 and add it to your AC against the attack.
Tales Of The Old Margreve - Players Guide

feature.name: Ranger Archetype: Spear of the Weald (totom)
feature.text: RESTRICTION: ALSEID - You can choose this archetype only if you are an alseid.

The alseid who inhabit the ancient Margreve forest have long defended the limits of their home. These forest warriors can make a dizzying variety of ranged and melee attacks in quick succession, using ancient magic to flash across the battlefield.
Tales Of The Old Margreve - Players Guide

feature.name: Weald Spear (Spear of the Weald) (totom)
feature.text: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you gain the ability to call forth a magical, wooden spear from the Bright Land into your empty hand as a bonus action. The spear disappears if it is more than 5 feet away from you for 1 minute or more. It also disappears if you use this feature again, if you dismiss the weapon (no action required), or if you die. You are proficient with the weapon while you wield it. The spear’s range is 20/60 feet, and, when you throw the spear, it reappears in your hand after the attack. The spear’s damage die is a d8, and it has the finesse and reach properties.

At 7th level, your weald spear attack counts as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage, and your Weald Spear’s damage die is a d10. At 11th level, your Weald Spear’s damage die is a d12.
Tales Of The Old Margreve - Players Guide

feature.name: Touch of the Bright Land (Spear of the Weald) (totom)
feature.text: The touch of the Bright Land is always on your spear, hinting at its otherworldly ties. Beginning at 3rd level, when you summon your Weald Spear, choose one of the following effects:
Entwined. Your spear appears to be wrapped in writhing green vines which occasionally coalesce into the shape of a slender, grasping hand. The hand points in the direction of the Margreve.

Aflame. Your spear is ensorcelled in heatless, white, magical flames whose intensity rises and falls to reflect your mood. When you are at your happiest, it sheds bright light in a 5-foot radius and dim light for an additional 5 feet.

Everblooming. Your spear is covered in small wildflowers that bloom, die, bud, and bloom again within minutes. Pollinating insects are often drawn to your spear as the spear constantly exudes a pleasant, floral fragrance.
Moonlit. Your spear appears as a pale, white length of wooden moonlight. A trail of star-like motes travels behind the spear’s point.
Tales Of The Old Margreve - Players Guide

feature.name: Canopy (Spear of the Weald) (totom)
feature.text: Beginning at 7th level, when a creature within 30 feet of you, including yourself, is targeted by a ranged weapon attack, you can use your reaction to summon a magical canopy of glowing leaves and branches over the target. The target has resistance to the damage dealt by the attack, and the canopy bursts into shredded leaves afterwards. You must then finish a short or long rest to use your Canopy again.
At 11th level, you can use your Canopy twice between rests. At 18th level, you can use it three times between rests. When you finish a short or long rest, you regain your expended uses.
Tales Of The Old Margreve - Players Guide

feature.name: Steps of the Forest God (Spear of the Weald) (totom)
feature.text: When you reach 11th level, you gain a minor use of the Elfroads. After you make a successful ranged weapon attack with your Weald Spear, you can use a bonus action to teleport to an unoccupied space within 10 feet of your target.
Tales Of The Old Margreve - Players Guide

feature.name: Overwhelm (Spear of the Weald) (totom)
feature.text: At 15th level, after you make a successful melee weapon attack with your Weald Spear against a creature, you can use a bonus action to make one ranged weapon attack with it against a different creature. You don’t have disadvantage on the ranged attack roll from being within 5 feet of the first creature, however, you can still have disadvantage on the attack roll from being within 5 feet of other creatures.
Tales Of The Old Margreve - Players Guide
